script.default: module.exports = function(client, scope, audience, context, cb) cb(null, access_token); };
module.exports = function(client, scope, audience, context, cb) cb(null, access_token); };

Expectation.description:
A canned response registered for one Management API operation,
optionally conditioned on the incoming request.

method.description: HTTP verb of the target operation.

path.description:
Target operation path — concrete (`/api/v2/users/auth0|1`) or an
OpenAPI template (`/api/v2/users/{id}`).

enrollment_message.default: {{code}} is your verification code for {{tenant.friendly_name}}. Please enter this code to verify your enrollment.
{{code}} is your verification code for {{tenant.friendly_name}}. Please enter this code to verify your enrollment.

script.default: module.exports = function(client, scope, audience, context, cb) cb(null, access_token); };
module.exports = function(client, scope, audience, context, cb) cb(null, access_token); };

RequestMatcher.description:
Optional conditions the incoming request must satisfy for the
expectation to apply. Omit it (or send `{}`) for a catch-all. `query`
and `body` are subset-matched: every key you specify must be present
and equal in the real request; extra keys are allowed. Arrays and
scalars must match exactly. The matcher is validated against the
operation's request schema (with `required` relaxed) at registration.

body.description: JSON the request body must contain (subset match).

query.description: Query parameters the request must carry.

ResponseDef.description: The canned response the mock returns when the expectation applies.

body.description:
Response body the mock should return. Validated against the target
operation's response schema for `status` before it is stored.

headers.description: Optional headers to attach to the response.

status.description: HTTP status code the mock should return.

enrollment_message.default: {{code}} is your verification code for {{tenant.friendly_name}}. Please enter this code to verify your enrollment.
{{code}} is your verification code for {{tenant.friendly_name}}. Please enter this code to verify your enrollment.
{{code}} is your verification code for {{tenant.friendly_name}}. Please enter this code to verify your enrollment.
{{code}} is your verification code for {{tenant.friendly_name}}. Please enter this code to verify your enrollment.

script.default: module.exports = function(client, scope, audience, context, cb) cb(null, access_token); };
module.exports = function(client, scope, audience, context, cb) cb(null, access_token); };

info.description:
**auth0-mock** is a drop-in mock of Auth0's Authentication and Management APIs. Run it locally and point your application's `AUTH0_DOMAIN` at it — your code calls auth0-mock the same way it calls Auth0, with real signed JWTs verifiable against the JWKS published at `/.well-known/jwks.json`.

This document covers every HTTP surface the mock exposes:

- **Authentication API** — `/oauth/token`, `/authorize`, `/userinfo`, `/v2/logout`, `/dbconnections/*`, `/passwordless/*`.
- **Management API** — every endpoint under `/api/v2` from the upstream Auth0 spec.
- **admin0** — control plane under `/admin0/*` for direct manipulation of in-memory state (registered matches, claim overlay, per-audience permissions, MFA-required flag).
- **service** — `/healthz`, `/.well-known/jwks.json`, `/openapi.json`, `/openapi.yaml`, `/docs`.

The **Try it** panel on this page is preloaded with a freshly-minted `client_credentials` token, so Management API calls succeed without any manual setup. The token's audience defaults to `DEFAULT_AUDIENCE` (`https://localhost:8443/api/v2/`) — override per-request from the auth selector if your test needs a different one.

Project source: <https://github.com/sergiught/auth0-mock>
info.title: auth0-mock API

get.summary: JSON Web Key Set used to verify tokens minted by /oauth/token.

delete.summary: Clear the global custom claim overlay.

get.summary: Read the global custom claim overlay applied to minted tokens.

put.summary: Replace the global custom claim overlay.

delete.description:
With an empty body, clears every registered expectation. With a
`{ "method": ..., "path": ... }` body, clears every expectation
registered for that operation (the catch-all and every
request-matched one).

400.description: Body was present but could not be parsed or was missing method/path.

delete.summary: Clear expectations — one (with a body) or all (with no body).

post.description:
The target operation is identified by `method` + `path`. `path` may be
concrete (`/api/v2/users/auth0|1`) or an OpenAPI template
(`/api/v2/users/{id}`); concrete registrations win over template ones
at request time. The optional `request` matcher conditions the
expectation on the incoming query + body — multiple expectations may be
registered per operation, the most specific match wins, ties broken by
newest. `response.body` is validated against the operation's response
schema for `response.status`, and the `request` matcher against the
operation's request schema, before anything is stored.

400.description: Body could not be parsed, names an unknown operation, or violates the response schema.

post.summary: Register (upsert) a canned response for a Management API operation.

200.description: Map of audience to permission list.

get.summary: List every audience that has a permissions overlay configured.

delete.summary: Clear the permissions overlay for one audience.

get.summary: Read the permissions overlay for one audience.

parameters.description:
API identifier — may include `/` characters. Chi mounts the
underlying handler with a `*` wildcard so the slashes round-trip; the
OpenAPI path uses the canonical `{audience}` form for tools that
can't represent wildcard paths.
parameters.name: audience

put.summary: Replace the permissions overlay for one audience.

post.summary: Wipe every store admin0 governs (expectations, claims, permissions, mfa-required).

302.description: Redirect to `redirect_uri` with `code` and `state` query parameters.

201.description: User created (mock returns a canned profile).

get.description:
Returns a single-page HTML reference that loads `/openapi.json` at
runtime and renders docs plus a "Try it out" panel. The spec's
`servers[0].url` already points at this mock, so requests issued from
the panel hit the same instance that served the page.
get.summary: Interactive API reference rendered by Scalar.

get.description: Returns 200 if the process is up. No authentication.

post.description:
Supports every grant type the mock implements: `client_credentials`,
`password`, `refresh_token`, `authorization_code` (with optional PKCE),
`http://auth0.com/oauth/grant-type/password-realm`, and the MFA
step-up grants (`mfa-otp`, `mfa-oob`, `mfa-recovery-code`).
post.summary: Exchange credentials for an access token.

get.description:
Returns the same bytes as `api/auth0-mock.openapi.json` in the repo.
Import this URL directly into Postman or Insomnia.
get.summary: The merged OpenAPI 3.1 spec for this mock instance.

200.description: OTP / link sent (mock returns 200 without sending).

post.summary: Verify a passwordless OTP and mint tokens.

get.summary: Resolve the authenticated user from an access token.
End the user's session and optionally redirect them.

tags.description: Service plumbing — health probe, JWKS, OpenAPI export, and the rendered docs page.
tags.name: Service

tags.description: OAuth2 / OIDC endpoints — token exchange, authorization-code flow, userinfo, OIDC discovery, logout, and token revocation.
tags.name: OAuth & OIDC

tags.description: Auth0 database-connection endpoints — user signup and password-change requests.
tags.name: Database Connections

tags.description: Auth0 passwordless flow — start (email/SMS OTP) and verify.
tags.name: Passwordless

tags.description:
Global custom-claim overlay. Claims set here are merged into every token
the mock mints, so tests can assert on claims without a real rule/action.
tags.name: Claims

tags.description:
Per-audience RBAC permission overlays. Lets tests pre-seed the
permissions a token carries for a given API identifier.
tags.name: Permissions

tags.description:
The mock-wide MFA-required flag. When set, password-style grants return
an `mfa_required` error so step-up flows can be exercised.
tags.name: MFA

tags.description:
Registered canned responses ("expectations") for Management API
operations, plus the global state reset. None of these require
authentication.
tags.name: Expectations
